title: Bothriochloa alta
intro: Bothriochloa alta, also known as buffalo grass, is a perennial grass species native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its strong rhizome system and tolerance to drought. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Bothriochloa alta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Bothriochloa alta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. Specifically, it belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae. This genus Bothriochloa is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. Bothriochloa alta is also known to be a type of perennial grass. It is often found in grasslands and open woodlands, particularly in areas with well-drained soils.
